wikidata_description: the item (institution, law, public office, public register, etc) or statement belongs to or has power over or applies to the value (a territorial jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)
description: A law, institution, public office, register, statement, or other head item applies to, belongs to, or exercises authority over the territorial jurisdiction represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: the political party of which a person is or has been a member or otherwise affiliated
description: The head person is or was a member of, or otherwise politically affiliated with, the political party represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: material or product, including services, produced or provided by an organization, industry, facility, or process
wikidata_aliases: product, material, or service produced or provided
description: The head organization, industry, facility, or process produces or provides the product, material, or service represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: person or organization for which the subject works or worked
description: The head person works or worked for the person or organization identified by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: founder or co-founder of this organization, religion, place or entity
description: The tail entity founded or co-founded the organization, religion, place, or other entity represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: league or competition in which team or player has played, or in which an event occurs
description: The tail entity is the league or competition in which the head team or player has participated, or in which the head event occurs.

wikidata_description: portion of a workforce population that is not employed
description: The tail value gives the unemployment rate for the population or territory represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: organization or person responsible for publishing a work, such as a book, periodical, printed music, podcast, game or software
description: The tail person or organization publishes the book, periodical, game, software, or other work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: owner of the subject
description: The tail person, organization, or other owner owns the head entity.

wikidata_label: located in the administrative territorial entity
wikidata_description: the item is located on the territory of the following administrative entity. Use P276 for specifying locations that are non-administrative places and for items about events. Use P1382 if the item falls only partially into the administrative entity
wikidata_aliases: is in the Indian reservation of
is in the Indian reserve of
is in the administrative region of
is in the local government area of
is in the rural city of
is in the principal area of
relation_name: located in the administrative territorial entity
description: The head entity is located within the administrative territory represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: administrative divisions that claim control of a given area
description: The tail administrative division or polity claims sovereignty or control over the territory represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: event in which a person, organization or creative work was/is a participant; inverse of P710 or P1923
description: The head entity participated in the event represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: creative work's genre or an artist's field of work (P101). Use main subject (P921) to relate creative works to their topic
description: The tail entity is the genre of the head creative work, or the artistic field in which the head artist works.

wikidata_description: person, state or item replaced. Use "structure replaces" (P1398) for structures. Use "follows" (P155) if the previous item was not replaced or predecessor and successor are identical
description: The head person, state, office, or item replaces the tail predecessor in the same role or function.

wikidata_description: other person or item which continues the item by replacing it in its role. Use P156 ("followed by") if the item is not replaced nor identical, but adds to the series (e.g. books in a series).
description: The tail successor replaces the head person, state, office, or item in the same role or function.

wikidata_description: person, profession, organization or entity that operates the equipment, facility, or service
description: The tail person, organization, profession, or other entity operates the equipment, facility, or service represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: country, state, department, canton or other administrative division of which the municipality is the governmental seat
description: The head municipality or settlement serves as the capital or governmental seat of the administrative division represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: religion of a person, organization or religious building, or associated with this subject
description: The head person, organization, or religious building is affiliated with or associated with the religion or worldview represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: language(s) that a person or a people speaks, writes or signs, including the native language(s)
description: The head person or people speaks, writes, or signs in the language represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: this (fictional or fictionalized) entity, place, or person appears in that work as part of the narration (use P2860 for works citing other works, P361/P1433 for works being part of other works, P1343 for entities described in non-fictional accounts)
description: The head fictional or fictionalized person, place, or entity appears as part of the narrative of the work represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: (list of) direct subdivisions of an administrative territorial entity
description: The head administrative territory contains the tail administrative territory as one of its direct subdivisions.

wikidata_description: immediately prior item in a series of which the subject is a part, preferably use as qualifier of P179 [if the subject has replaced the preceding item, e.g. political offices, use "replaces" (P1365)]
description: The head entity immediately follows or succeeds the tail entity in an ordered sequence.

wikidata_description: immediately following item in a series of which the subject is a part, preferably use as qualifier of P179 [if the subject has been replaced, e.g. political offices, use "replaced by" (P1366)]
description: The tail entity immediately follows or succeeds the head entity in an ordered sequence.

wikidata_description: city or town where an organization's headquarters is or has been situated. Use P276 qualifier for specific building
description: The tail city, town, or other place is where the head organization has or had its headquarters.

wikidata_description: actor in the subject production [use "character role" (P453) and/or "name of the character role" (P4633) as qualifiers] [use "voice actor" (P725) for voice-only role] - [use "recorded participant" (P11108) for non-fiction productions]
description: The tail performer appears as a cast member in the film, television production, stage work, or other production represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: person(s) who produced the film, musical work, theatrical production, etc. (for film, this does not include executive producers, associate producers, etc.) [for production company, use P272, video games - use P178]
description: The tail person produced the film, musical work, theatrical production, or similar work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
description: The head person, organization, or creative work received the award or recognition represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: sovereign state that this subject item is in (not to be used for human beings)
description: The tail sovereign state is the country to which the head item belongs or in which it is located.

wikidata_description: maker of this creative work or other object (where no more specific property exists)
description: The tail person or organization created the work, product, or other entity represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: closest parent taxon of the taxon in question
description: The head taxon is classified directly below the parent taxon represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: subject's ethnicity (consensus is that a VERY high standard of proof is needed for this field to be used. In general this means 1) the subject claims it themselves, or 2) it is widely agreed on by scholars, or 3) is fictional and portrayed as such)
description: The tail entity is the ethnicity explicitly claimed by or reliably established for the head entity.

wikidata_description: actor, musician, band or other performer associated with this role or musical work
description: The tail actor, musician, band, or other performer is associated with the role or musical work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: (main or final) manufacturer or producer of this product
description: The tail person or organization manufactures the product or physical item represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: organization or person that developed the item
description: The tail person or organization developed the software, game, product, or other work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: series which contains the subject
description: The head item belongs to the series represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: most specific known birth location of a person, animal or fictional character
description: The tail entity is the most specific known location where the head person, animal, or fictional character was born.

wikidata_description: twin towns, sister cities, twinned municipalities and other localities that have a partnership or cooperative agreement, either legally or informally acknowledged by their governments
description: The head and tail local administrative bodies have a twinning, sister-city, or comparable cooperative partnership that is legally or informally acknowledged by their governments.

wikidata_description: legislative body governing this entity; political institution with elected representatives, such as a parliament/legislature or council
description: The tail entity is the legislative body of the country, state, municipality, or other political entity represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: most specific known (e.g. city instead of country, or hospital instead of city) death location of a person, animal or fictional character
description: The tail entity is the most specific known location where the head person, animal, or fictional character died.

wikidata_description: country that have drainage to/from or border the body of water
description: The body of water represented by the head entity drains through, receives drainage from, or borders the tail country.

wikidata_label: located in or next to body of water
wikidata_description: body of water on or next to which a place is located
wikidata_aliases: located next to body of water
relation_name: located in or next to body of water
description: The head geographic entity is located in or adjacent to the body of water represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: male parent of the subject. For stepfather, use "stepparent" (P3448)
description: The tail person is the father of the head person.

wikidata_description: branch to which this military unit, award, office, or person belongs, e.g. Royal Navy
description: The head person, military unit, or organization belongs to the armed-service branch represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: female parent of the subject. For stepmother, use "stepparent" (P3448)
description: The tail person is the mother of the head person.

wikidata_description: the subject has the object as their spouse (husband, wife, partner, etc.). Use "unmarried partner" (P451) for non-married companions
description: The head person and the tail person are spouses or were married to each other.

wikidata_description: brand and trademark associated with the marketing of subject music recordings and music videos
description: The tail record-label brand or trademark is associated with marketing the music recording or music video represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: the object is a country that recognizes the subject as its citizen
description: The head person holds or held citizenship of the country represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: company that produced this film, audio or performing arts work
description: The tail company produced the film, television program, game, or other creative work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: location of the object, structure or event; use P131 to indicate the containing administrative entity, P8138 for statistical entities, or P706 for geographic entities; use P7153 for locations associated with the object
description: The tail entity is the non-administrative physical location of the head object or structure, or the place where the head event occurred.

wikidata_description: this item is a subclass (subset) of that item; ALL instances of this item are instances of that item; different from P31 (instance of), e.g.: volcano is a subclass of mountain; Everest is an instance of mountain
wikidata_aliases: all members of this class also belong to
whose instances are a subset of those of
description: The head class is a subclass whose instances are also instances of the broader class represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: continent of which the subject is a part
description: The head geographic entity forms part of the continent represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different from P279 (subclass of); for example: K2 is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
description: The head entity is a particular instance or example of the class represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: the subject and the object have at least one common parent (brother, sister, etc. including half-siblings); use "relative" (P1038) for siblings-in-law (brother-in-law, sister-in-law, etc.) and step-siblings (step-brothers, step-sisters, etc.)
description: The head person and the tail person share at least one parent and are therefore siblings.

wikidata_description: official with the highest formal authority in a country/state
description: The tail official holds the highest formal authority in the country or state represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: child organization/unit of an org./unit; usually a fully owned separate org.; for non-independent sub-units, see org. division (P199); opposite of parent org./unit (P749); use instance of (P31) to distinguish org. (Q43229) and org. unit (Q10387680)
description: The tail organization or unit is a child or subsidiary organization of the head organization or unit.

wikidata_description: seat of government of a country, province, state or other type of administrative territorial entity
description: The tail city or place serves as the capital of the country, state, or administrative entity represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: object of which the subject is a part (if this subject is already part of object A which is a part of object B, then please only make the subject part of object A), inverse property of "has part" (P527, see also "has parts of the class" (P2670))
description: The head entity is a component or constituent part of the larger entity represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_label: original language of film or TV show
wikidata_description: language in which a film or a performance work was originally created. Deprecated for written works and songs; use P407 ("language of work or name") instead.
relation_name: original language of film or TV show
description: The tail language is the language in which the head film or performance work was originally created.

wikidata_description: language designated as official by this item
description: The tail language has official status in the country, region, organization, or other entity represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: subject currently or formerly holds the object position or public office
description: The head person holds or formerly held the office or position represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: subject has object as child. Do not use for stepchildren—use "relative" (P1038), qualified with "kinship to subject" (P1039)
description: The tail person is a child of the head person.

wikidata_description: platform for which a work was developed or released, or the specific platform version of a software product
description: The head work was developed or released for the tail platform, or the head software product has the tail as a specific platform version.

wikidata_description: the body of water to which the watercourse drains
description: The head watercourse drains into the body of water represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: network(s) or service(s) that originally broadcast a radio or television program
description: The tail network or service originally broadcast the radio or television program represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: organization, club or musical group to which the subject belongs. Do not use for membership in ethnic or social groups, nor for holding a political position, such as a member of parliament (use P39 for that)
description: The head person or organization is a member of the organization, group, or body represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: presiding member of an organization, group or body
description: The tail person chairs the organization, committee, or body represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: country of origin of this item (creative work, food, phrase, product, etc.)
description: The head work, product, or other entity originated in the country represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: main creator(s) of a written work (use on works, not humans); use P2093 (author name string) when Wikidata item is unknown or does not exist
description: The tail person wrote or authored the work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: part of this subject; inverse property of "part of" (P361). See also "has parts of the class" (P2670).
description: The head entity includes the tail entity as one of its parts or components.

wikidata_description: sports teams or clubs that the subject represents or represented
description: The head athlete or player is or was a member of the sports team represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: the place where the person is or has been, resident
description: The head person resides or formerly resided at the location represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: date on which the subject was born
description: The tail date or time specifies when the head person was born.

wikidata_description: director(s) of film, TV-series, stageplay, video game or similar
description: The tail person directed the film, television program, stage work, game, or similar work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: date on which the subject died
description: The tail date or time specifies when the head person died.

wikidata_description: time when an entity begins to exist; for date of official opening use P1619
description: The tail date or time marks when the head entity began to exist, was founded, or was created.

wikidata_description: point in time at which the subject (organisation, building) ceased to exist; see "date of official closure" (P3999) for closing a facility, "service retirement" (P730) for retiring equipment, "discontinued date" (P2669) for stopping a product
description: The tail date or time marks when the head organization, building, or other entity ceased to exist through dissolution, abolition, or demolition.

wikidata_description: date or point in time when a work or product was first published or released
description: The tail date or time specifies when the head work or product was first published or released.

wikidata_description: person(s) who wrote the script for subject item
description: The tail person wrote the screenplay or script for the work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: time an entity begins to exist or a statement starts being valid
description: The tail value specifies when the head entity, event, role, or statement began to exist or apply.

wikidata_description: moment when an entity ceases to exist and a statement stops being entirely valid or no longer be true
description: The tail value specifies when the head entity, event, role, or statement ceased to exist or apply.

wikidata_description: date something took place, existed or a statement was true; for providing time use the "refine date" property (P4241)
description: The tail value specifies when the head event occurred, the head entity existed, or the head statement was true.

wikidata_description: head of the executive power of this town, city, municipality, state, country, or other governmental body
description: The tail person leads the executive government of the city, state, country, or other governmental body represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: battles, wars or other military engagements in which the person or item participated
description: The head person, organization, or other entity took part in the battle, war, or other military conflict represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: characters which appear in this item (like plays, operas, operettas, books, comics, films, TV series, video games)
description: The tail fictional character appears in the play, book, film, television series, game, or other work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: author of song lyrics
description: The tail person wrote the lyrics of the song or musical work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: educational institution attended by subject
description: The head person attended or was educated at the educational institution represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: located on the specified (geo)physical feature. Should not be used when the value is only political/administrative (P131) or a mountain range (P4552). Use P206 for things in/on bodies of water.
description: The head geographic entity is located on the physical geographic feature represented by the tail entity; this does not cover purely administrative locations, mountain ranges, or bodies of water.

wikidata_description: person, group of people or organization (object) that actively takes/took part in an event or process (subject). Preferably qualify with "object has role" (P3831). Use P1923 for participants that are teams.
description: The tail person, group, or organization actively participated in the event or process represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: the subject (person, idea, etc.) was influenced or inspired by this object entity, e.g. "Heidegger was influenced by Aristotle"
description: The tail person, idea, or other entity influenced or inspired the head person, idea, or entity.

wikidata_description: location where a group or organization was formed
description: The head group or organization was formed at the location represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: parent organization or unit of an organization or unit, opposite of child organization or unit (P355); use instance of (P31) to distinguish organization (Q43229) and organization unit (Q10387680)
description: The tail organization or unit is the parent organization that owns, contains, or controls the head organization or unit.

wikidata_description: notable scientific, artistic or literary work, or other work of significance among subject's works
description: The tail work is a notable scientific, artistic, literary, or otherwise significant work created by the head person or organization.

wikidata_description: subject was founded or started by separating from identified object
description: The head entity was founded or began by separating from the entity represented by the tail.

wikidata_description: the narrative of the work is set in this location
description: The narrative of the head work is set in the location represented by the tail entity.

wikidata_description: person(s) who wrote the music [for lyricist, use "lyrics by" (P676)]
description: The tail person composed the music for the work represented by the head entity.

wikidata_description: location where persons or organisations were actively participating in employment, business or other work
description: The tail location is where the head person or organization actively works or conducts employment or business.
